blocks.content: First just a few at the edge of the field where the grass met the tree line. Then more. Then the whole field beginning to blink — slow and unhurried, like the field itself was breathing.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Eli Turner has always had a vision. People are only now recognizing it.
Kit had gone quiet. He was sitting on the edge of a blanket with his elbows on his knees, watching the field. Daisy sat down next to him. Not touching — just close.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was thinking about the summer we were twelve. This field. You and me and Eli and Savannah running around until somebody's parents called us in.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Your mom called you in at nine-thirty and you acted like it was a personal offense.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It was nine-thirty. We were in the middle of something.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We were lying in the grass looking at the sky.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We were in the middle of something.
She smiled. Around them, the field kept filling with light — the field full of fireflies.
